comment: Exact label match; both denote the administrator role/person in conference management.
Exact label match; both classes represent authors of submitted conference contributions/papers.
Exact label match; both represent a paper submission, though ConfOf models Paper specifically as a kind of Contribution while CMT models Paper as a kind of Document.
Exact label match; both denote a conference, but the surrounding class structure differs across ontologies.
Exact label match; both represent persons involved in the conference domain.
Subject area in CMT closely corresponds to topic in ConfOf as the thematic area of a paper or event, but labels are not identical and scope may differ slightly.
ProgramCommitteeMember in CMT is close to Member_PC in ConfOf; both denote program committee members, though ConfOf emphasizes reviewing and expertise restrictions.

CMT Reviewer is close to ConfOf Member_PC because ConfOf annotates Member_PC as Reviewer and constrains it to review contributions; however, CMT distinguishes reviewer from program committee member more explicitly.
Program committee chair in CMT closely corresponds to Chair_PC in ConfOf.
Both datatype properties denote the title of a submitted paper/contribution; labels differ syntactically but semantics are highly similar.
Both datatype properties represent an email address for a person.
CMT name and ConfOf hasTitle are only loosely related as textual naming properties; domains differ substantially, so this is not an equivalence.
Both properties link a paper/contribution to its thematic area or topic.
Both properties connect a paper/contribution to its author; domains and ranges are closely aligned though the surrounding class hierarchies differ.
Both properties relate an author to a paper/contribution they write.
